reviews.review_msg: He just reads the notes for lectures, which are just extracts of the textbook copied verbatim, but without context. Filled notes & tutorial solutions are not posted, forcing attendance to see the answers. The midterm was a huge difficulty spike, but the final was fair. Make sure you can do all tutorial & assignment problems that are doable by hand.

Worst class I've ever taken. Professor Tsai would read/copy everything straight from the textbook and oftentimes was confused about his own notes. It can be difficult to follow lectures as he goes over everything very fast and it can be hard to read his handwriting. The content wasn't too bad, but the way it was explained made it 10x more confusing
Prof. Tsai genuinely cares about his students and truly wants to see them succeed. He goes offers great advice on co-ops and career decisions. He is approachable, patient, and always willing to help.
He is very hard to understand and his notes are awful! Notes are literally  copied word to word from text book and he just read  from that!
This professor is hard to understand based off the way he teaches. He reads off the notes with very little explanation. His exams are very difficult.

Professor Tsai is one of the most caring professors I have ever met at SFU. He genuinely cares about his students and is willing to help them if they have any difficulties. He tests you on what he teaches, so there are no surprises on his exams.

His notes are mostly proofs. they help a bit. Focus on understanding his assignments & practice tests. He's very fair & available to help if you're stuck. There's no surprises on tests if you've been studying the homework. He actually cares a lot & is very understanding. When students had problems he would always listen and try to accommodate us.
Very helpful prof, knows his stuff well.
Good prof, all his material is very technical, so learning to understand and deriving formulas helps. Beware of accent, sometimes hard to comprehend :P
Pretty straight forward.  To learn the material, you should rederive the formulas yourself until you understand them.  For exams, study his examples well.  He will not use any tricks.
Very nice man, and helpful lecture
